opinion.h1: MURPHY v. COMMISSIONER OF EDUCATION, 361 U.S. 117 (1959)
opinion.h4: MURPHY v. COMMISSIONER OF EDUCATION OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 419. Decided November 23, 1959.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
361 U.S. 117 BREATON v. UNITED STATES. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SIXTH CIRCUIT. No. 54, Misc. Decided November 23, 1959.
Certiorari granted; judgment vacated and case remanded for consideration in light of Public Law 86-320, 73 Stat. 590.
Solicitor General Rankin for the United States.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the District Court for further consideration in the light of Public Law 86-320, approved September 21, 1959, 73 Stat. 590.
